name: Flesh of the Pig Ala Bob Greenberg (and Secret Sauce)
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------SAUCE FOR RE-HEATING----------------------------
1. Open an A-1 Sauce bottle and a small sauce bottle labeled "Open Pit."
2. Add ¼ cup of molasses to the sauce.
3. Prepare a supply of hickory chips for cooking.
To prepare ribs:
1. Trim excess fat from spare, country, or other pork ribs (not too much fat).
2. Cut ribs into even thickness pieces.
3. Place ribs in a large baking pan and pour cider vinegar over them.
4. Sprinkle garlic salt and black pepper evenly over the ribs.
5. Ensure all sides are coated; avoid using pepper mills.
Cooking:
1. Use a moderate-hot fire with the ribs at least a couple of inches away from direct heat.
2. Avoid covering the grill to prevent flare-ups.
3. Turn the ribs frequently until they turn brown and reach desired doneness.
4. Do not baste or burn the meat.
Sauce preparation:
1. Reheat the sauce (A-1 and Open Pit bottles with molasses) over low heat until it begins to boil slowly.
2. Add cold pork from the refrigerator.
3. Alternatively, wrap ribs in foil and reheat at 325°F for 15-20 minutes without sauce.
Store any remaining sauce and reuse later; note that it will contain pork fat.
